channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: German voters prepare to elect a new parliament. Here's what to expect on and after election day
item.description: German voters go to the polls Sunday to elect a parliament that will determine who runs the country for the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Geir Moulson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas to return 4 Israeli bodies, including a mother and her 2 young children
item.description: Hamas is set to return the bodies of four Israeli hostages, including a mother and her two children who have long been feared dead and had come to embody the nation’s agony following the Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Jahjouh, Wafaa Shurafa And Melanie Lidman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea's Yoon is in court for a hearing in his criminal trial
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol is in court for a hearing where his lawyers are contesting his arrest on a criminal charge alleging he was orchestrating a rebellion when he imposed martial law.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italy PM Meloni visits pope in hospital, says he's in good spirits as tests show slight improvement
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis for 20 minutes in the hospital Wednesday and reported he was “alert and responsive” and full of good humor, despite his diagnosis of pneumonia and a complicated bronchial infection that has sidelined the 88-year-old pontiff for six days
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, ANDREA ROSA and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rahm Emanuel: Trump helping achieve Stalin’s dream with Russia-Ukraine comments
item.description: Former U.S. Ambassador to Japan Rahm Emanuel said President Trump is helping to achieve former Soviet Union dictator Joseph Stalin’s dream with his recent actions surrounding the Russia-Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Israel confirms 4 dead hostages who will be returned from Gaza include young family
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The remains of the youngest and one of the oldest captives seized by Hamas during the attack that ignited the war in the Gaza Strip are expected to be returned on Thursday, an Israeli official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Advance of Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo threatens regional peace, UN officials warn
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Top United Nations officials in Africa warned an emergency meeting of the U.N. Security Council on Wednesday that the offensive of Rwanda-backed rebels in mineral-rich eastern Congo is threatening peace in the wider region.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 8 in 10 say Putin should not be trusted: Survey
item.description: The Quinnipiac University Poll released Wednesday found 81% of American voters believe Putin should not be trusted, while Trump's administration attempts to broker a peace deal to end the conflict between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: Experts say a combination of training and technology were critical to all 76 passengers and four crew members surviving the fiery Delta Air Lines plane crash in Toronto.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexico president says her government requested US surveillance drone flights
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Wednesday that surveillance drone flights by the U.S. government over Mexico are occurring in collaboration with and at the request of her government. They come as U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A look at the 8 Latin American crime groups designated as terrorist organizations by the US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The United States government is formally designating eight Latin American organized crime groups that also operate in the U.S. to be “foreign terrorist organizations.” They are involved in drug trafficking, migrant smuggling and extend their territory through violence.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement, receives visit from Italian PM Giorgia Meloni
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis in the hospital on Wednesday where she spent 20 minutes with the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas to turn over bodies of Shiri Bibas and 2 young children
item.description: Israel has confirmed the identities of the four deceased hostages set to be released on Thursday as Shiri Bibas, her two children, and Oded Lifshitz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson: Trump's Ukraine comments meant to 'shock' Europe into action
item.description: Former U.K. Prime Minister Boris Johnson said President Trump’s recent remarks about Ukraine are intended to “shock” Europe into action.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Italian Prime Minister Meloni visits Pope Francis in hospital, says he's in good spirits
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis in hospital on Wednesday and reported he was full of good humor following his diagnosis of pneumonia and a bronchial infection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze halted a crucial program fighting HIV in Africa. Here's what's at stake
item.description: The freeze on U.S. foreign aid ordered by President Donald Trump has effectively halted one of the world's most successful responses to a disease.
item.dc:creator: Farai Mutsaka And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A worried NATO holds large-scale combat drills as the US stance on Europe shifts under Trump
item.description: SMARDAN, Romania (AP) — NATO members continued their largest combat exercises of 2025 on Wednesday, testing their ability to rapidly deploy large-scale forces on the 32-nation alliance’s eastern border as worries grow over its most powerful member, the United States.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and NICOLAE DUMITRACHE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis slept peacefully, ate breakfast following diagnosis of pneumonia in both lungs
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, slept well and ate breakfast on Wednesday morning after he was diagnosed with bilateral pneumonia, according to the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police in disputed Kashmir raid bookstores, seizing books related to Islamic group
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Police in Indian-controlled Kashmir raided bookstores and seized hundreds of books linked to a major Islamic organization in the disputed region, where strict controls on the press have escalated in recent years. The raids began on Friday in Srinagar, the region’s main city.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s prosecutor-general files charges against ex-President Bolsonaro over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Prosecutor-General Paulo Gonet has filed charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro, alleging he attempted a coup to stay in office following his 2022 election loss.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet of a Philippine patrol plane over the disputed Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea; the plane did not have to alter its path to avoid a collision.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French bill would ban headscarves in sport; Amnesty International says it's discriminatory
item.description: Amnesty International is urging French lawmakers to reject a bill to ban religious clothing and symbols, including headscarves, during competitions, saying it is discriminatory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Attacks by Sudanese RSF paramilitaries leave hundreds dead in White Nile State
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Attacks by Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Force have killed hundreds of civilians, including infants, in White Nile state, Sudanese officials and rights groups said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea vows to expand nuclear forces, blasts US for 'outdated' denuclearization plan
item.description: North Korea vowed to expand its nuclear forces under Kim Jong Un, criticizing the U.S. and its neighbors in Asia for pushing an "absurd" denuclearization plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Toronto plane crash: Harrowing video shows Delta plane erupting into fireball, flipping upside down
item.description: All 80 people aboard the Delta plane that flipped upside down and crash landed in Toronto survived, but three, including a child, are critically injured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US citizen detained on drug smuggling charges in Russia released amid talks between Moscow, Washington
item.description: A U.S. citizen arrested in Russia earlier this month on drug smuggling charges was released ahead of talks in Saudi Arabia between Moscow and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Russian, US officials meet in Saudi Arabia to begin talks on Ukraine war without officials from Kyiv
item.description: Senior Russian and American officials met in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday to begin talks on improving relations and negotiating an end to Moscow's war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 18 killed in stampede at New Delhi railway station in India as Hindu pilgrims travel to festival
item.description: In New Delhi, India, at least 18 people were killed in a stampede at a railway station. Many victims were Hindu pilgrims traveling to the Maha Kumbh festival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Some white South Africans showed support for President Donald Trump on Saturday and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: ALFONSO NQUNJANA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation
